major_tropes: The Hidden World Beneath the Mundane
The Protagonist Who Straddles Both Worlds
The Half-Breed or Outsider Who Belongs Nowhere
plot_template: A person with one foot in the mundane world and one in the magical discovers that a threat originating in the supernatural world is about to spill into — or destroy — the human one. They must navigate conflicting loyalties, a fractured magical society, and their own uncertain identity to prevent catastrophe.
character_archetypes: The Half-Aware Protagonist With Hidden Power
The Cynical Experienced Guide Who Has Stopped Caring
The Faction Leader Whose Goals Overlap With the Protagonist's For Now
The Monster Who Is Not Monstrous
world_building_notes: The magic system must be integrated into the modern city, not just layered on top. Factions (vampires, fae, witches, etc.) have politics, economies, and grudges. The masquerade creates constant tension — how do magical events get explained? Who does the covering up and why? The city itself should feel different at night.
emotional_core: Identity, belonging in two worlds, the weight of hidden knowledge, power and its price
